author: Robert Carr
description: A hot oven-finished meatball sub with tender beef-pork meatballs, quick tomato sauce, garlic-Parmesan toasted rolls, provolone, mozzarella, and fresh basil.
equipment: Medium saucepan or small Dutch oven

groceryIngredients.item: Italian sub roll or sturdy hoagie roll

ingredients: 1 1/2 ounces Parmesan cheese, finely grated to make about 1/2 cup, divided
1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 3/4 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1/2 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
2 tablespoons fresh basil leaves, torn, divided
2 Italian sub rolls or sturdy hoagie rolls, 6 to 8 inches long, split
2 ounces low-moisture whole-milk mozzarella cheese, shredded, about 1/2 cup
instructions: Move an oven rack 6 inches below the broiler and heat the broiler on high.
Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminum foil and brush the foil with 2 teaspoons extra-virgin olive oil.
In a mixing bowl, stir together 1/4 cup panko breadcrumbs and 2 tablespoons whole milk.
Let the panko and milk sit for 5 minutes so the breadcrumbs soften into a panade.

Add 8 ounces 80/20 ground beef, 4 ounces ground pork, 1 large egg yolk, 1/4 cup finely grated Parmesan cheese, 1 finely grated garlic clove, 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley, 1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, 1/2 teaspoon dried oregano, 1/4 teaspoon lightly crushed fennel seeds, and 1/2 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce to the softened panko.
Mix the meatball mixture gently with your hands just until the beef, pork, egg, cheese, garlic, parsley, and seasonings are evenly distributed.
Divide the meatball mixture into 8 meatballs, using about 2 tablespoons mixture for each meatball.
Roll each meatball lightly so it holds together without being packed tight, then place the 8 meatballs on the oiled foil.
Broil the meatballs for 7 to 9 minutes, rotating the baking sheet once, until the tops are browned in spots; the centers do not need to be fully cooked yet.
While the meatballs broil, warm the remaining 1 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil in a medium saucepan or small Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the finely diced yellow onion, 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and 1/8 teaspoon black pepper to the hot oil.
Cook the onion for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring often, until softened and lightly golden at the edges.
Stir in 1 minced garlic clove, 1 tablespoon tomato paste, and the remaining 1/4 teaspoon dried oregano.
Cook the garlic and tomato paste for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the tomato paste darkens slightly and smells savory.
Add the 14.5-ounce can crushed tomatoes, 2 tablespoons water, the remaining 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce to the saucepan.
Bring the sauce to a steady simmer over medium heat, stirring the bottom of the pan so the tomato paste dissolves into the crushed tomatoes.
Transfer the browned meatballs and any juices from the foil into the simmering sauce.
Reduce the heat to medium-low, partially cover the saucepan, and simmer the meatballs in the sauce for 10 to 12 minutes.
Gently turn the meatballs once halfway through simmering so all sides are coated in sauce.
Check that the center of the largest meatball reaches 160 degrees F on an instant-read thermometer.
Stir 1 tablespoon finely grated Parmesan cheese, 1/2 teaspoon red wine vinegar, and 1 tablespoon torn fresh basil into the sauce.
Reduce the sauce to low heat so the meatballs stay hot while the rolls toast.
Discard the first sheet of foil from the baking sheet, line the baking sheet with fresh foil, and place the 2 split sub rolls cut-side up on the clean foil.
In a small bowl, stir together 1 tablespoon softened unsalted butter, the remaining 1 finely grated garlic clove, and 1 tablespoon finely grated Parmesan cheese.
Spread the garlic-Parmesan butter evenly over the cut sides of the 2 split rolls, using about 3/4 teaspoon butter mixture on each cut side.
Broil the buttered rolls for 1 to 2 minutes, watching constantly, until the cut sides are lightly toasted around the edges.
Move the 2 toasted top rolls to a plate and leave the 2 toasted bottom rolls cut-side up on the baking sheet.
Place 1 slice provolone cheese on each toasted bottom roll.
Use a slotted spoon to place 4 hot meatballs on each bottom roll.
Spoon about 1/3 cup hot tomato sauce over the meatballs on each sandwich.
Tear the remaining 2 slices provolone cheese over the sauced meatballs, dividing 1 slice provolone per sandwich.
Sprinkle 1/4 cup shredded low-moisture mozzarella cheese and 1 tablespoon finely grated Parmesan cheese over each open sandwich.
Broil the open sandwiches for 2 to 3 minutes, until the provolone and mozzarella are melted and lightly browned in spots.
Scatter the remaining 1 tablespoon torn fresh basil over the hot cheese.
Close the sandwiches with the toasted top rolls and serve immediately while the meatballs, sauce, and cheese are hot.
notes: This sandwich is hot by temperature, not spice; the flavor comes from browning, garlic, Parmesan, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, fennel, basil, and melted cheese.
Broiling the meatballs first gives fast browned edges, then the sauce finishes them gently so they stay juicy.
Use 80/20 ground beef and ground pork. Very lean beef can make the meatballs firm before the sauce has time to season them.
Mix the meatball mixture gently and stop as soon as it looks even. Overmixed meatballs can turn springy.
Lightly crushed fennel seeds give the meatballs a subtle Italian sausage flavor without needing store-bought sausage.
The rolls should be sturdy enough to hold hot sauce. Soft sandwich rolls work if they are toasted well before filling.
If the sauce bubbles aggressively after the meatballs are added, lower the heat so the meatballs simmer instead of bouncing around and breaking.
The final broil should be quick. Pull the sandwiches as soon as the cheese melts and browns in spots so the bread stays crisp instead of drying out.
Serve right away with extra tomato sauce on the side for spooning over each bite.
title: Skillet Meatball Subs
